posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
My recent delivery…thanks Nick!

  • 2014 Domaine Jacques Carillon Puligny-Montrachet - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Puligny-Montrachet (11/18/2016)
    Pale yellow, green tint color…nose of citrus orchard, sea breeze, crushed limestone…crystaline pureness here! More laser beam structure than I’m use to with the wines from Louis and Francois…in fact I kept thinking Les Perrieres! Crystal clean orchard fruits…intense citrus cut, crunchy and saline minerals, slight flint strike, rain water, white flowers. With the all the structure, it still possesses a nice cream to the freshened palate…finished with great mineral spice, and an instate urge for another sip! These Carillon’s can bring it! (93 pts.)

Posted from CellarTracker

1024×768 173 KB

posts.name: Robert Grenley
posts.cooked: For those who have experienced them, how would you compare Jacques and Francois Carillon and how they are comparing with the Louis Carillon wines?

posts.name: G. Shields
posts.cooked:
Great note! As the French say, ‘ca donne très envie!’
Am really hoping I can get my hands on some of either of the carillon’s wines from 2014, but yet to see any in the cavistes in Lyon.
I get the feeling that as burg volumes are low, what little there is gets shipped to the US and not much stays in France! There seem to be distribution timing differences: hardly any 2014s available before now while notes appearing since spring from overseas.

posts.name: patrick c albright
posts.cooked:
Gawain,
It appears that the wines from Francois are much more prevalent in Lyon.
Fazeli may have some on Maréchal de Saxe.

Patrick

posts.name: G. Shields
posts.cooked: Cool! Thanks for the tip Patrick, will check them out to see if they have any. Cheers!

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: The best village Puligny on the market for mine. Thanks for the note Mr Buzz.
Jacques wines are more mineral than the wines from Louis.

posts.name: Nick Gangas
posts.cooked: love these wines. My favorite producer in Puligny.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
2014 was a real success here: a vintage which synergized totally with the kind of wines Jacques likes to make. I tasted a few last month after working through the '15s and was really impressed.

And entirely agree with Jeremy that this is a strong contender for the best village Puligny to be found. Vicent Dureuil-Janthial’s Corvée des Vignes bottling is also up there for me in the last few vintages. Lamy’s Haute Densité Puligny ‘Les Tremblots’ might surpass them all however.

posts.name: John W Osgood
posts.cooked: Thanks for the note Buzz. This bottle is also a favorite of mine. Can’t wait to get my hands on the 14s!

posts.name: patrick c albright
posts.cooked:
William Kelley:

. Lamy’s Haute Densité Puligny ‘Les Tremblots’ might surpass them all however.

Well yes . . . but apples and oranges in availability, price, and production levels.
